sections.paragraph:
συμπαθής, ές (since Eur., s. below; Aristot. et al.; CIG 9438; OGI 456, 66; LXX, Philo) sympathetic, understanding (Eur., TGF 164 ἄριστον ἀνδρὶ κτῆμα συμπαθὴς γυνή=‘the best thing for a husband is an understanding wife’; Polyb. 2, 56, 7; 8, 22, 9; Plut., Eum. 594 [18, 5], Mor. 536a; Jos., Ant. 19, 330) 1 Pt 3:8.—TW. Spicq.

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

CIG CIG = Corpus Inscriptionum Graecarum—List 3

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

TGF TGF = Tragicorum Graecorum Fragmenta—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 958
